title: Encryption Posture Ledger
description: SSOT for every persistent store and cross-component connection Soleur operates. Layer A (scripts/lint-encryption-posture.py) resolves every citation in this file against real code on every CI run; Layer B reconciles the live_verification: available rows against actual provider/host state. See ADR-139 and knowledge-base/project/plans/2026-07-23-feat-encryption-posture-design-time-default-plan.md (Plan Review Revisions R1-R11).

store_classes.description: R7: the CHECKED partition. A Terraform resource type here is treated as a persistent store and MUST carry a ledger row. Extending this is the single place a new store class enters.

non_store_types.description: R7: the ACKNOWLEDGED-non-store partition, seeded from the repo's current Terraform resource-type inventory. A resource type in neither store_classes nor here is FAIL-CLOSED ("add <type> to store_classes or non_store_types").

non_iac_stores.description: R8: stores with NO *.tf anchor (Supabase, Doppler, Better Stack, the R2 state backend, session Redis). The expected-count floor counts these explicitly so deleting a row cannot silently lower the floor.

store.description: Resource address (hcloud_volume.x / cloudflare_r2_bucket.y) or logical name for a non-IaC store (supabase.prd).

device_binding.description: R1: REQUIRED for guest-luks-volume. Names the volume resource address AND its hcloud_volume_attachment, so the LUKS apparatus is reached from the attachment, NEVER from a name-similarity match against a sibling volume. Prevents a mechanism:luks row on the plaintext hcloud_volume.workspaces false-PASSing on hcloud_volume.workspaces_luks's apparatus.

mapper.description: The resolved /dev/mapper/<name> name; must match the luksOpen operand (after one level of ${VAR:-default} resolution) AND the mount/gate.

mechanism.description: luks | provider-managed:<named attestation> | app-layer-envelope:<scheme> | plaintext-exception. NEVER 'the provider handles it'.

evidence.description: Mechanically-resolvable citation. For luks it is implied by device_binding. For provider-managed: attestation name + URL. For app-layer-envelope: file:anchor.

retrieved_on.description: R9: ISO date the attestation was retrieved. Layer A FAILs when older than 365 days (offline date arithmetic).

does_not_defend.description: R10: mandatory; a reviewer judges semantics. Empty / none / n/a are rejected. (The verbatim-restatement regex was deleted per R10 as a vacuous grep for a semantic property.)

disclosed_as.description: R5: the docs/legal/** file:anchor making a public claim about this store, or the literal not-publicly-claimed. Layer A FAILs a plaintext-exception / cert_verification:off row whose disclosed_as resolves to text asserting encryption.

live_verification.description: R2/R8: available => a host/provider probe exists and the positive-work floor counts it; unavailable:<reason> => no permitted probe (its own tracking issue).

cert_verification.description: on | off. off REQUIRES an exception block. (The ban-list scan that catches rejectUnauthorized:false / sslmode=require lives in Semgrep per R6, not here.)

exception.description: Present ONLY when mechanism is plaintext-exception OR cert_verification is off. R3: expires_on is Layer-A-enforced (<=90 days); an expired exception FAILs the required check. accepted_by was DELETED per R3 (agent-authored control with one value in a one-person shop).
